unit.scope: Client vitest 1361/1361 (125 files). New MarkdownRenderer.test.tsx (3): pop-out button renders when onPopOut is provided; clicking invokes onPopOut and never calls window.open; button hidden when onPopOut omitted (modal-nested instance). SmartViewer.test.tsx gains 2: popOut={false} suppresses the pop-out button; clicking pop-out opens the centered modal (smart-viewer-modal), never calls window.open, and leaves exactly one pop-out control in the DOM (nested SmartViewer renders none). doc-sync gains the SmartViewerModal token (component_inventory.md updated). Server unchanged (client-only iterate).

e2e.scope: Playwright (2 specs, localhost-pinned worktree Vite stack on :5191, file API route-mocked). (1) smartviewer-popout-modal.spec.ts (NEW) via /preview: clicking the pop-out button opens a viewport-centered Radix Dialog modal (modal center within 8px of the viewport center on both axes; height > 85vh), the header shows the file path, the modal body renders a nested SmartViewer (document-markdown visible), the nested pop-out button is suppressed (count 0 inside the modal), ESC closes the modal, the close-X closes it, and window.open is never called (no new tab). (2) smartviewer-document-render.spec.ts regression: the pop-out button is still visible on /preview after the change. Real Chromium.

design_fidelity.scope: Modal geometry (viewport-centered, 90vh, portal-to-body dimmed backdrop) asserted via boundingBox math in real Chromium (E2E) plus a captured screenshot confirming the header (file path + close X), the nested SmartViewer (PathStrip + body), and the dimmed full-app backdrop.

smoke.scope: Client-only iterate (a React component + prop threading). No server / API change. Vite stack booted only to drive the F0.5 E2E.

surface_verification.justification: UI behavior change (pop-out new-tab -> centered in-app modal); surface=web drove two real-Chromium Playwright specs against a live localhost worktree stack. The new spec asserts centered geometry, nested-pop-out suppression, ESC/X close, and zero window.open calls; the existing document-render spec confirms no regression. Production client build (npm run build) also passed, validating the SmartViewer<->SmartViewerModal render-time circular import under Rollup.
